name: Edelman's
description_short: Family-owned precious metals and collectibles buyer/seller established in 1926, operating in Jenkintown and Abington, PA with collateral-based loans and fair market appraisals.
description_long:
Edelman's is a fourth-generation family-owned business founded by Louis Edelman in 1926 as a loan office. The company has operated continuously for nearly 100 years under the foundational principles of fairness, honesty, and customer respect. Located in the Philadelphia area (Jenkintown and Abington, PA), Edelman's has built its reputation on transparent dealings and refusing to apply pressure tactics to customers.

The company operates across three main service lines: buying and selling precious metals (gold and silver bullion), numismatic and collectible coins, and collateral-based loans. They purchase gold jewelry, silver flatware, paper money, and various collectibles including Franklin Mint sterling. On the sales side, they offer competitive bullion pricing positioned as lower than national online retailers, serving both new investors and experienced collectors. Expert appraisals are conducted on-site with transparency throughout the valuation process.

Edelman's distinguishes itself through explicit commitments to fairness-based pricing, promise-keeping regardless of loss, and quality customer service. The fourth-generation owner emphasizes that the company will mail refund checks if items of significant value are discovered after a transaction. They explicitly state they match competitor prices and never renege on agreed deals. Customer testimonials highlight lack of pressure, thorough explanations, and emotional comfort during transactions—important for customers unfamiliar with precious metals markets.

As a traditional pawn and collectibles shop, Edelman's operates in a narrow geographic footprint (two PA locations) and relies heavily on in-person appraisals, which limits accessibility for remote customers. While they position competitive pricing, verification of claims against national retailers cannot be confirmed from website content alone. The business model depends on foot traffic and local market reputation rather than digital-first engagement.
pros: Nearly 100-year operating history (since 1926) demonstrates stability and market credibility
Fourth-generation family ownership with documented commitment to fairness and honesty
Promises to mail refund checks if items of significant value are missed during initial appraisal
Explicitly match competitor prices and never renege on agreed deals
No-pressure sales environment with in-person transparent assessments
Broad product acceptance: gold, silver, bullion, coins, paper money, jewelry, collectibles
Collateral loan services available in addition to buy/sell services
cons: Limited geographic footprint (only Jenkintown and Abington, PA) restricts customer access
No online appraisal or remote transaction options mentioned—requires in-person visits
Competitive pricing claims ('lower than national online retailers') are unverified on website
No transparent pricing structure or fee schedule disclosed on website
Limited information about loan terms, interest rates, or collateral requirements for loan services
best_for: Pennsylvania residents in the Philadelphia metro area seeking in-person precious metals transactions with accountability
Investors diversifying portfolios with physical gold/silver bullion at local brick-and-mortar pricing
Individuals with inherited jewelry, coins, or collectibles needing fair professional appraisals
Customers uncomfortable with online transactions who value personal relationships and transparent dealings
diagnosis: Edelman's is best for Pennsylvania customers seeking trustworthy, relationship-based precious metals transactions with in-person accountability and fair pricing. The main caveat is geographic limitation—the business serves only two PA locations and offers no remote services, making it inaccessible to customers outside the immediate area.
services: Coin buying and selling (numismatic and bullion coins)
Collectibles purchasing (sterling silver flatware, hollowware, Franklin Mint items)
Jewelry buying (old gold and silver jewelry)
meta_description: Family-owned precious metals and collectibles buyer/seller since 1926 in PA offering collateral loans.
